item_a.name: Casper Original Mattress
item_a.image_alt: Casper Original Mattress with three layers of foam on white bed frame
pros: Excellent edge support for a foam mattress
Zoned support for targeted pressure relief
Responsive feel that's easier to move on
Strong reputation with physical retail presence
cons: Shorter trial period than Nectar (100 vs 365 nights)
Less generous warranty (10 years vs lifetime)
Higher price point for similar construction
item_a.best_for: Combination sleepers who change positions frequently and want a balanced, responsive feel with good edge support.

item_b.name: Nectar Memory Foam Mattress
item_b.image_alt: Nectar Memory Foam Mattress with quilted cover and gel memory foam layers
pros: Lower price point with frequent promotions
Deep memory foam contouring for pressure relief
cons: Slower response time may feel too sinky for stomach sleepers
Weaker edge support compared to competitors
Takes longer to break in (30+ days)
item_b.best_for: Side sleepers and budget-conscious buyers who want deep pressure relief with exceptional trial and warranty terms.

comparison_summary: Both Casper and Nectar offer quality all-foam mattresses, but with distinct differences. Nectar provides better value with its lower price, 365-night trial, and lifetime warranty, making it ideal for risk-averse buyers. Casper counters with superior edge support, more responsive foam, and zoned support that better serves combination sleepers who change positions throughout the night.
verdict_text: Nectar edges out Casper for most buyers due to its exceptional value, industry-leading trial period, and lifetime warranty, though Casper remains the better choice for combination sleepers prioritizing responsiveness.
seo_content:
When comparing Casper vs Nectar, you're looking at two of the most recognizable names in the bed-in-a-box industry, each having helped revolutionize how Americans buy mattresses. Both offer all-foam construction with memory foam comfort layers, but their approaches to sleep differ in meaningful ways that affect who should buy which mattress.

The Casper Original Mattress features a three-layer design with AirScape perforated foam for breathability, zoned support targeting shoulders and hips, and a durable base layer. At 12 inches thick and weighing 71 pounds in a queen, it offers a balanced medium-firm feel (6/10) with quicker response times than traditional memory foam. The zoned support system is particularly noteworthy—it provides softer cushioning under shoulders while maintaining firmer support under the hips and lumbar region. This makes Casper especially suitable for combination sleepers who shift positions throughout the night.

The Nectar Memory Foam Mattress also stands 12 inches tall but takes a different approach with its five-layer construction. It features a quilted gel memory foam cover, three inches of gel memory foam, a transition layer, and a dense support core. Weighing 84 pounds in queen size, Nectar offers deeper contouring and pressure relief with its slower-responding memory foam. This classic memory foam feel creates that signature "hugging" sensation that side sleepers and pressure-point sufferers often prefer.

In the Nectar vs Casper debate, value proposition plays a significant role. Nectar typically costs $200-300 less than Casper for equivalent sizes, and the value extends beyond initial price. Nectar's 365-night trial period is unmatched in the industry—giving you a full year to decide if the mattress works for you, compared to Casper's still-generous 100 nights. The lifetime warranty from Nectar versus Casper's 10-year coverage adds another layer of long-term value and peace of mind.

However, Casper justifies its premium with tangible performance differences. Edge support is noticeably stronger on the Casper, meaning you can utilize the full surface area without feeling like you might roll off. The quicker response time makes it easier to change positions and get in and out of bed—a consideration often overlooked until you experience a memory foam mattress that's too slow. Casper's established retail presence also means you can try before you buy at numerous physical locations.

For temperature regulation, both mattresses incorporate gel infusions and breathable covers, though neither excels for hot sleepers compared to hybrid or latex options. Both work well on adjustable bases and various foundations.

Ultimately, choosing between these mattresses depends on your sleep style and priorities. Nectar delivers exceptional value with industry-leading policies for side sleepers and budget-conscious buyers. Casper offers a more versatile, responsive feel with better edge support for combination sleepers willing to invest more upfront.

faq.q: Which mattress is better for side sleepers, Casper or Nectar?
faq.a: Nectar is generally better for dedicated side sleepers due to its deeper memory foam contouring that cushions shoulders and hips more effectively. The slower-responding foam creates more pressure relief at these key points. However, if you're a combination sleeper who also spends time on your back or stomach, Casper's more responsive feel and zoned support may serve you better.

faq.q: Does Nectar or Casper sleep cooler?
faq.a: Both mattresses sleep similarly warm as all-foam constructions with gel infusions. Casper's AirScape perforated foam provides slightly better airflow, but neither is ideal for hot sleepers. If temperature regulation is a priority, consider hybrid versions from either brand (Casper Wave Hybrid or Nectar Premier Hybrid) which incorporate coils for better breathability.

faq.q: Is the Nectar 365-night trial really worth it over Casper's 100 nights?
faq.a: Yes, especially considering memory foam mattresses can take 30-60 days to fully break in and for your body to adjust. The 365-night trial eliminates buyer's remorse and gives you time through all seasons to evaluate comfort. Many people don't realize a mattress isn't working until several months in, making Nectar's extended trial a significant advantage.

faq.q: Which mattress has better edge support, Casper or Nectar?
faq.a: Casper has noticeably better edge support, rating approximately 7.5/10 compared to Nectar's 6/10. Casper's perimeter reinforcement allows you to sleep or sit near the edge without excessive compression. Nectar's edges compress more significantly, which is common for memory foam mattresses but means you lose some usable surface area.

faq.q: Can heavier people use Casper or Nectar mattresses?
faq.a: Both mattresses can accommodate heavier individuals up to approximately 300-350 pounds per person, but neither is optimal for sleepers over 250 pounds. Heavier individuals may experience faster sagging and reduced support with all-foam constructions. For those over 230 pounds, consider hybrid models or mattresses specifically designed for heavier sleepers with denser support cores and reinforced edges.

meta_title: Casper vs Nectar (2026): Which Mattress Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Casper vs Nectar mattresses. We break down price, comfort, trial periods, warranties, and sleep performance to help you pick the right bed-in-a-box.
